body:
## Summary
- Add a local Quartz Hypothesis component/plugin that injects the official browser embed only when `HYPOTHESIS_ENABLED=true`.
- Support build-time options for sidebar state, highlights, comments mode, group allowlist, and theme.
- Pass Hypothesis repository variables into the site validation build and document setup in the README.
- Keep agent-facing Hypothesis API access out of this PR; that should be handled separately as a Sepo runtime capability.

## Verification
- `npm run check`
- `npm run build`
- `HYPOTHESIS_ENABLED=true HYPOTHESIS_OPEN_SIDEBAR=true HYPOTHESIS_COMMENTS_MODE=true HYPOTHESIS_GROUPS_ALLOWLIST=abc123,def456 HYPOTHESIS_THEME=clean npm run quartz -- build`
- Confirmed disabled output has no Hypothesis embed/config and enabled output includes the embed loader/config.

Refs: https://h.readthedocs.io/projects/client/en/latest/publishers/embedding.html, https://h.readthedocs.io/projects/client/en/latest/publishers/config.html

Closes #8

comments.body:
## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->
<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis-head: d5e5dc863f1779a4adc75f9bac6729af6fcd2f25 -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #10 adds opt-in Hypothesis web annotations to the Quartz site, controlled by build-time repository or hosting variables.
- The PR intentionally narrows issue #8 to browser-side site annotation support after discussion that agent-facing Hypothesis API access should be handled separately.
- The implementation follows the existing local Quartz component/plugin pattern and keeps Hypothesis API tokens out of browser-side configuration.
- The current PR state has no existing inline review comments or unresolved review threads.

## Review
The current review artifacts do not support any unresolved required branch change. The main option-validity warning from one reviewer is not carried forward because the current official Hypothesis config docs document `commentsMode`, `groupsAllowlist`, and boolean `showHighlights`.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| No actionable issues found | INFO | Current review artifacts and PR state do not support a required branch change before merge. |

## Progress
- Confirmed issue #8 was narrowed to site-side Hypothesis embed support; agent API access remains intentionally out of scope.
- Verified current Hypothesis docs list the PR’s documented options: https://h.readthedocs.io/projects/client/en/latest/publishers/config.html
- Existing inline PR comments and review threads were empty; no inline comments were posted, replied to, minimized, or resolved.
- The rubrics review noted `npm run check` passed and later saw third-party plugin restore/build failures, but did not provide evidence tying those failures to this Hypothesis change.

## Recommended Next Step
NO_AUTOMATED_ACTION: There is no unresolved actionable branch-change work supported by the current review artifacts or PR state.

## Final Verdict
SHIP

## Action Items
- [ ] No required branch changes.

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `xhigh`_

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 10 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | Issue #8 was narrowed to site-side build-time Hypothesis support, and the PR uses the official Hypothesis embed/config contract: JSON config plus `https://hypothes.is/embed.js`. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | Reuses existing Quartz component registry/env parsing patterns in [`quartz.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/d5e5dc863f1779a4adc75f9bac6729af6fcd2f25/quartz.ts#L119-L143) and the existing after-DOM-loaded script pattern. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Diff is scoped to the Hypothesis component/plugin, build env passthrough, lock entry, and README setup docs; no broad refactor or unrelated behavior changes. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | Implementation is a small local component plus one inline loader: [`Hypothesis.tsx`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/d5e5dc863f1779a4adc75f9bac6729af6fcd2f25/quartz/components/Hypothesis.tsx#L14-L44) and [`hypothesis.inline.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/d5e5dc863f1779a4adc75f9bac6729af6fcd2f25/quartz/components/scripts/hypothesis.inline.ts#L1-L19). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | README documents enablement, optional variables, and the no-browser-token boundary in [`README.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/d5e5dc863f1779a4adc75f9bac6729af6fcd2f25/README.md#L149-L168). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Minimal defaults, native escape hatches | pass | 8/8 | Built-in support stays opt-in through `HYPOTHESIS_ENABLED`; advanced agent API access is explicitly left to a separate Sepo runtime capability. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | Uses separate validated env fields for enablement, sidebar, highlights, comments mode, group allowlist, and theme in [`quartz.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/d5e5dc863f1779a4adc75f9bac6729af6fcd2f25/quartz.ts#L119-L131). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Name config by capability | pass | 6/6 | Public variables are named for the general Hypothesis capability, e.g. `HYPOTHESIS_ENABLED`, `HYPOTHESIS_GROUPS_ALLOWLIST`, and `HYPOTHESIS_THEME`. |
| communication / generic | Make setup guidance actionable | pass | 7/7 | README gives the required `gh variable set HYPOTHESIS_ENABLED --body true --repo OWNER/REPO` command and lists optional build variables. |
| communication / generic | Write audience-first documentation | pass | 7/7 | Docs lead with the user task of enabling annotations, then list optional settings and the token boundary without front-loading implementation internals. |

## Notes

- The PR matches the clarified scope from issue #8: build-time-enabled browser annotation support only, with agent-facing API access kept out of this PR.
- Independent verification passed with Node 22/npm 10: `npm ci`, `npm run check`, `npm run build`, and an enabled Hypothesis build. Disabled output had no Hypothesis markers; enabled output included the JSON config and embed loader.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific findings.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `high`_

title: Add build-time Hypothesis site annotations
